name: Honey
description: Sweet golden honey gathered from sparkling flower beds and Combee habitats. A versatile crafting and cooking ingredient beloved by Bug-type Pokemon.
howToObtain: Interact with sparkling flower beds in any area, or collect from Combee habitat Litter boxes.
farmingTip: Build Combee habitats early and check their Litter regularly. Sparkling flower beds respawn daily across all four main areas.

name: Sturdy Stick
description: A solid wooden stick found while exploring or chopping wood. One of the most basic and frequently needed crafting materials in Pokopia.
howToObtain: General exploring drops, use Cut on wood objects, or collect from Cacturne drops.
farmingTip: Cut every piece of wood you see in Withered Wasteland. Cacturne drops these reliably if you have one as a companion.

name: Stone
description: A plain chunk of stone found everywhere. Essential building block for walls, paths, and countless crafting recipes.
howToObtain: General exploring drops, use Rock Smash on stone blocks, or collect from Rock-type Pokemon Litter boxes.
location: All main areas, especially Rocky Ridges
farmingTip: Rock-type Pokemon habitats produce Stone in their Litter constantly. Rock Smash every block you pass in Rocky Ridges for bulk gathering.

name: Leaf
description: A fresh green leaf gathered from vegetation. Used in basic crafting recipes and some early cooking dishes.
howToObtain: General exploring drops, use Cut on tall grass and vines, or collect from Venusaur drops.
farmingTip: Withered Wasteland and Bleak Beach have abundant tall grass. Venusaur is an excellent farming companion for Leaf drops.

name: Small Log
description: A short log cut from trees and worn lumber. The primary source material for Lumber when processed by a Pokemon with the Chop specialty.
howToObtain: Use Cut on trees and worn lumber throughout all areas. Also drops from Haxorus and appears in Haxorus Litter boxes.
farmingTip: Haxorus Litter boxes are the best passive source. For active farming, cycle through trees in Withered Wasteland since they regrow quickly.

name: Vine Rope
description: Strong natural rope made from cut vines. Used in furniture crafting, bridges, and various construction recipes.
howToObtain: Use Cut on vines growing on walls and structures. Bellsprout and Weepinbell also drop Vine Rope.
farmingTip: Vines regrow after a day. Bellsprout habitats with Litter boxes provide a steady passive supply without needing to explore.

name: Glowing Mushroom
description: A bioluminescent mushroom found in dark caves. Its soft glow makes it useful in lighting recipes and certain special crafts.
howToObtain: Found growing in caves throughout the Withered Wasteland and Rocky Ridges areas.
location: Caves in Withered Wasteland and Rocky Ridges
farmingTip: Cave mushrooms respawn regularly. The Wasteland Dream Island also has abundant Glowing Mushrooms in its underground sections.

name: Twine
description: Thin, sturdy string recovered from textile garbage or spun by Bug-type Pokemon. A staple crafting material for nets, furniture, and decorations.
howToObtain: Collect from textile garbage piles, Bug-type Pokemon Litter boxes, or by cutting cobwebs in caves.
farmingTip: Bug-type habitats are the most reliable source. Cutting cobwebs in caves yields Twine as a bonus while mining.

name: Sea Glass Fragment
description: Smooth, frosted glass pieces found on polluted coastal tiles. Once cleaned, they become beautiful crafting materials for decorations and coastal furniture.
howToObtain: Found on muk-covered tiles at Bleak Beach. Clean up pollution to reveal the fragments beneath.
farmingTip: Systematically clean muk-covered tiles at Bleak Beach. Each cleaned tile has a chance to drop Sea Glass. Ocean Dream Island is another reliable source.

name: Seashell
description: A pretty shell washed ashore on the beach. Used in coastal-themed decorations and some crafting recipes.
howToObtain: Pick up along the shoreline at Bleak Beach.
farmingTip: Walk the full Bleak Beach shoreline each day. Shells respawn daily and are plentiful once you clear the pollution.

name: Squishy Clay
description: Soft, malleable clay dug from clay blocks. When processed with the Burn specialty, it becomes sturdy Brick for construction.
howToObtain: Break clay blocks found in various areas, or collect from Ground-type Pokemon Litter boxes.
farmingTip: Ground-type Litter boxes are the easiest passive source. Dig up clay blocks whenever you spot them to stockpile for Brick production.

name: Fluff
description: Soft, fluffy wool gathered from electric sheep Pokemon. Used in textile crafting recipes for furniture cushions and soft decorations.
howToObtain: Collect from Mareep and Flaaffy habitat Litter boxes.
farmingTip: Build Mareep habitats as soon as possible. Their Litter boxes produce Fluff consistently, making it one of the easiest materials to farm passively.

name: Wastepaper
description: Discarded paper scraps found among the urban ruins. Can be recycled into clean Paper for crafting wallpapers and decorations.
howToObtain: Found while exploring the Sparkling Skylands area.
farmingTip: Sparkling Skylands is loaded with Wastepaper. Collect it in bulk and Recycle it into Paper for wallpaper and decoration recipes.

name: Seaweed
description: Aquatic plant matter gathered from water areas. Used in certain crafting recipes and some cooking dishes.
howToObtain: Collected from water areas, particularly at Bleak Beach and anywhere you can Surf.
location: Bleak Beach, water areas in all regions
farmingTip: Once you learn Surf from Lapras at Bleak Beach, water areas across all regions become accessible for Seaweed gathering.

name: Nonburnable Garbage
description: Trash and debris cluttering the polluted beach. Can be recycled into useful Iron Ore rather than simply discarded.
howToObtain: Clean up pollution at Bleak Beach.
farmingTip: Do not throw this away. Use the Recycle ability to convert Nonburnable Garbage into Iron Ore, giving you a valuable secondary ore source.

name: Copper Ore
description: Raw copper extracted from copper-veined rock blocks in the mountains. Smelted into Copper Ingots for tools and decorations.
howToObtain: Mine copper blocks in Rocky Ridges using Rock Smash.
location: Rocky Ridges, Rock Peak Dream Island
farmingTip: Rocky Ridges has abundant copper blocks on its lower levels. Rock Peak Dream Island also has copper deposits for supplemental farming.

name: Iron Ore
description: Dense iron ore mined from the deeper sections of Rocky Ridges. The backbone of mid-game crafting when smelted into Iron Ingots.
howToObtain: Mine iron blocks in Rocky Ridges, or Recycle Nonburnable Garbage from Bleak Beach.
farmingTip: Combine active mining in Rocky Ridges with Recycling Nonburnable Garbage from Bleak Beach for maximum Iron Ore throughput.

name: Gold Ore
description: Precious gold ore found in the deepest mining areas. Smelted into Gold Ingots for premium furniture and decorations.
howToObtain: Mine gold blocks in the deeper sections of Rocky Ridges.
farmingTip: Gold blocks are rarer than copper or iron. Focus on the deepest mine levels in Rocky Ridges. Volcanic Dream Island has additional gold deposits.

name: Pokemetal Fragment
description: A mysterious metallic fragment unique to Pokopia. Found in special Pokemetal blocks that only appear in the most extreme environments. Smelted into Pokemetal for top-tier crafting.
howToObtain: Mine Pokemetal blocks found in Withered Wasteland and Sparkling Skylands.
location: Withered Wasteland, Sparkling Skylands, Sky Dream Island
farmingTip: Pokemetal blocks are scattered across Withered Wasteland and Sparkling Skylands. Sky Dream Island is the best Dream Island source. Smelt these into Pokemetal for endgame recipes.

name: Rare Pokemetal Fragment
description: An exceptionally pure piece of Pokemetal that occasionally drops from Pokemetal blocks. Extremely valuable and needed for the rarest crafting recipes.
howToObtain: Low drop rate from mining Pokemetal blocks in Withered Wasteland and Sparkling Skylands.
farmingTip: This is a rare drop, not guaranteed. Mine every Pokemetal block you find and be patient. You will need several for legendary-tier recipes.

name: Lumber
description: Processed wooden planks created when a Pokemon with the Chop specialty works on Small Logs. The primary building material for wooden structures and furniture.
howToObtain: Use the Chop specialty (Scyther or Haxorus) on Small Logs.
farmingTip: Recruit Scyther or Haxorus as soon as possible for their Chop specialty. Keep a stockpile of Small Logs feeding into Lumber production at all times.

name: Brick
description: A sturdy fired brick made by applying the Burn specialty to Squishy Clay. Essential for stone buildings, ovens, and industrial structures.
howToObtain: Use the Burn specialty on Squishy Clay.
farmingTip: Ground-type Litter boxes produce Squishy Clay passively. Keep a Burn-specialty Pokemon busy converting clay to bricks for your construction projects.

name: Crystal Fragment
description: A shimmering piece of crystal broken from the large crystal formations in the mountains. Used in premium decorations and special crafting recipes.
howToObtain: Mine large crystal formations found in Rocky Ridges.
farmingTip: Large crystals in Rocky Ridges are the primary source. Sky Dream Island also has Crystal Fragments for supplemental farming.

name: Copper Ingot
description: A refined copper bar produced by smelting Copper Ore. Used in wiring, decorative fixtures, and mid-tier crafting recipes.
howToObtain: Smelt Copper Ore at a furnace or with a Smelting specialty Pokemon.
farmingTip: Set up a smelting station near your ore storage. Process copper in batches to keep your ingot supply ahead of your crafting needs.

name: Iron Ingot
description: A solid iron bar smelted from Iron Ore. The workhorse metal for tools, structural supports, and industrial crafting. Tinkaton can process these into Tinkagears.
howToObtain: Smelt Iron Ore at a furnace or with a Smelting specialty Pokemon.
farmingTip: Iron Ingots are in constant demand. Maintain a smelting pipeline from Rocky Ridges mining runs and Bleak Beach garbage recycling.

name: Gold Ingot
description: A gleaming gold bar smelted from Gold Ore. Used in premium furniture, luxury decorations, and some late-game crafting recipes.
howToObtain: Smelt Gold Ore at a furnace or with a Smelting specialty Pokemon.
farmingTip: Gold Ingots are needed less frequently than Iron but in larger quantities per recipe. Stockpile Gold Ore during mining runs.

name: Pokemetal
description: A refined bar of the mysterious Pokemetal unique to Pokopia. Required for the most advanced crafting recipes and endgame structures.
howToObtain: Smelt Pokemetal Fragments at a furnace or with a Smelting specialty Pokemon.
farmingTip: Pokemetal Fragments are scarce. Mine every Pokemetal block in Withered Wasteland, Sparkling Skylands, and Sky Dream Island to keep your supply up.

name: Paper
description: Clean, usable paper recycled from Wastepaper. Used in wallpaper recipes, decorations, and various crafting projects.
howToObtain: Use the Recycle ability on Wastepaper collected from Sparkling Skylands.
farmingTip: Sparkling Skylands has Wastepaper everywhere. Gather in bulk and recycle it all at once for efficient Paper production.

name: Glass
description: Clear glass produced by applying the Burn specialty to volcanic ash, sand, or sandstone. Used in windows, lanterns, and decorative crafting.
howToObtain: Use the Burn specialty on volcanic ash, sand, or sandstone blocks.
farmingTip: Sand and sandstone are plentiful in Bleak Beach and Rocky Ridges. Volcanic ash appears near lava areas. Keep a Burn-specialty Pokemon assigned to glass production.

name: Tinkagear
description: A precision-crafted gear component produced exclusively by Tinkaton processing Iron Ingots. Required for mechanical contraptions and advanced machinery recipes.
howToObtain: Have Tinkaton process Iron Ingots using its unique specialty.
farmingTip: Tinkaton is the only Pokemon that can make Tinkagears. Recruit one as soon as possible and keep it supplied with Iron Ingots.

name: Concrete
description: A heavy, durable building material produced by mixing Limestone in a Concrete Mixer. Essential for urban-style construction in Sparkling Skylands and Palette Town builds.
howToObtain: Process Limestone in a Concrete Mixer. Limestone is found in Sparkling Skylands.
farmingTip: Limestone is specific to Sparkling Skylands and Rock Peak Dream Island. Build a Concrete Mixer early in the Skylands phase to start stockpiling.

name: Aspear Berry Seeds
description: Seeds that grow into Aspear Berry plants. Aspear Berries are a key cooking ingredient used in several recipes.
howToObtain: Found while exploring, purchased from shops, or obtained from harvested Aspear Berry plants.
farmingTip: Plant these using Rototiller-prepared soil. Water daily for best growth. Save some berries to get more seeds rather than using them all for cooking.

name: Chesto Berry Seeds
description: Seeds for growing Chesto Berries, a dry berry used in cooking recipes and valued by certain Pokemon as gifts.
howToObtain: Found while exploring or purchased from shops.
farmingTip: Chesto Berries grow quickly and are useful in early cooking recipes. Plant a row alongside your other berry crops.

name: Leppa Berry Seeds
description: Seeds that produce Leppa Berries, a sweet red berry frequently used in cooking and as Pokemon gifts.
howToObtain: Found while exploring, especially in Wasteland Dream Island.
farmingTip: Wasteland Dream Island is particularly rich in Leppa Berry Seeds. Grab extras during Dream Island visits.

name: Pecha Berry Seeds
description: Seeds for growing sweet Pecha Berries. A staple cooking ingredient and popular gift for Pokemon.
howToObtain: Found while exploring or purchased from shops.
farmingTip: Pecha Berries are among the most used cooking ingredients. Dedicate a farm plot to continuous Pecha production.

name: Wheat Seeds
description: Seeds for growing wheat, a fundamental crop used in bread, pastries, and many cooking recipes.
howToObtain: Found while exploring or purchased from shops.
farmingTip: Wheat is used in a huge number of cooking recipes. Plant large fields and keep a constant supply growing. Use Rototiller to prepare soil efficiently.

name: Tomato Seeds
description: Seeds that grow into tomato plants. Tomatoes are a versatile cooking ingredient used in sauces, salads, and main dishes.
howToObtain: Found while exploring or purchased from shops.
farmingTip: Tomato plants produce multiple harvests before needing replanting. Keep them watered daily for maximum yield.

name: Bean Seeds
description: Seeds for growing beans, a nutritious crop used in hearty cooking recipes.
howToObtain: Found while exploring or purchased from shops.
farmingTip: Beans grow reliably and are used in several recipes that Chef Dente teaches at Rocky Ridges. Always keep some in stock.

name: Stardust
description: Shimmering cosmic dust found only on Dream Islands. A premium crafting material needed for the most special recipes and decorations in Pokopia.
howToObtain: Collected exclusively from Dream Islands. Cannot be found in the four main areas or Palette Town.
farmingTip: You can only visit one Dream Island per day via Drifloon. Plan your visits strategically and always gather all available Stardust during each trip.

name: Rainbow Feathers
description: Magnificent iridescent feathers shed by Ho-Oh. Among the rarest materials in all of Pokopia, used in legendary-tier crafting recipes.
howToObtain: Dropped by Ho-Oh. Requires building the Altar of Flame habitat in Palette Town to attract Ho-Oh.
farmingTip: Ho-Oh must be attracted by building and completing the Altar of Flame in Palette Town. Rainbow Feathers drop infrequently, so be patient.

name: Silver Feathers
description: Ethereal silver feathers from Lugia, shimmering with oceanic energy. Required for some of the most powerful crafting recipes in the game.
howToObtain: Dropped by Lugia. Requires building a special ocean habitat to attract Lugia.
location: Palette Town or Bleak Beach area
farmingTip: Lugia is one of the hardest legendaries to attract. Build the required habitat and check regularly for Silver Feather drops.

name: Mysterious Slate
description: An ancient stone tablet inscribed with mysterious symbols. There are 27 total hidden throughout Pokopia, and collecting all of them is the key to encountering Mew.
howToObtain: Found hidden throughout all areas of Pokopia. 27 total scattered across the game world.
location: All areas (27 total throughout the game)
farmingTip: These are fixed collectibles, not farmable. Explore every corner of every area thoroughly. Some are hidden behind puzzles or in hard-to-reach spots. Collecting all 27 unlocks Mew.

name: Strange Strings
description: Unusual glowing filaments recovered from rare glowing blocks. Their origin is unknown, but they are prized by collectors and crafters alike.
howToObtain: Rare drop from glowing blocks found in caves and underground areas.
farmingTip: Glowing blocks are uncommon and the drop rate for Strange Strings is low. Mine every glowing block you encounter across all areas.

name: Armor Fragment
description: A piece of ancient armor material found in rare glowing blocks. Used in special crafting recipes for durable structures and decorations.
howToObtain: Rare drop from glowing blocks found in caves and underground areas.
farmingTip: Like Strange Strings, these drop from glowing blocks at a low rate. Prioritize mining any glowing blocks you spot for a chance at this rare material.
